Patch Set 14:

Before this patch series gets too far down the road, I'd prefer that the "find" and "semantic traverse" code is moved to a separate liblustreapi_find.c file, instead of continuing to add to liblustreapi.c.

Patch Set 14:

Mind if I do that in a patch at the end?  I'll make a single _find.c including pfind and find

Patch Set 14:

as we gro the tools more and more with more and more comlicated functionality, I wonder if it's worth it to rewrite / add some new advanced tools written in something like python that abstracts away a bunch of the complications?

Obviously we cannot do this with the underlying libraries, but things like parallel find and other parallel stuff would be much easier to do there.

A side bonus might be that we'll have a nice first-party python library to interact with Lustre that might be useful for other projects that are python heavy (like e.g. in ML space)?
Patch Set 14: Code-Review+2

parents.subject: LU-17055 mdt: add fallocate(FALLOC_FL_ZERO_RANGE) for indirect

commit.subject: LU-17814 utils: implement thread pool
commit.message:
LU-17814 utils: implement thread pool

Implement thread pool for parallel find - still uses
regular find code to do the actual find.

Test-Parameters: trivial
Signed-off-by: Patrick Farrell <pfarrell@whamcloud.com>
